lede: A look at February 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Devin White and AB de Villiers, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: February 2016 Ankara bombing
events.desc: Military vehicles explode outside a Turkish Armed Forces barracks in Ankara, Turkey, killing at least 29 people and injuring 61 others.

events.title: Arab Spring
events.desc: Arab Spring: Libyan protests against Muammar Gaddafi's regime begin.

events.title: 1996 Biak earthquake
events.desc: The 8.2 Mw Biak earthquake shakes the Papua province of eastern Indonesia with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). A large tsunami followed, leaving 166 people dead or missing and 423 injured.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: The Provisional IRA detonates an incendiary bomb at the La Mon restaurant, near Belfast, killing 12 and seriously injuring 30 others, all Protestants.

events.title: Jeffrey R. MacDonald
events.desc: The family of Jeffrey R. MacDonald, United States Army captain, is found murdered in their home in Fort Bragg, North Carolina, US. Eventually, MacDonald was charged with and convicted of the murder of his pregnant wife and two daughters.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Eniwetok begins. The battle ends in an American victory on February 22.

events.title: Armory Show
events.desc: The Armory Show opens in New York City, displaying works of artists who subsequently become some of the most influential painters of the early 20th century.

events.title: Geneva
events.desc: A group of citizens of Geneva found an International Committee for Relief to the Wounded, which later became known as the International Committee of the Red Cross.

events.title: Cochinchina campaign
events.desc: Cochinchina Campaign: The French Navy captures the Citadel of Saigon, a fortress manned by 1,000 Nguyễn dynasty soldiers, en route to conquering Saigon and other regions of southern Viet Nam.

events.title: Myles Standish
events.desc: Myles Standish is appointed as first military commander of the English Plymouth Colony in North America.

births.name: Billie Joe Armstrong
births.line: American singer-songwriter, guitarist, actor, and producer

births.name: Aryeh Deri
births.line: Moroccan-Israeli rabbi and politician, Israeli Minister of Internal Affairs

deaths.text: Jesse Jackson, American civil rights leader, activist, and politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Seven Founders of the Servite Order Alexis Falconieri
Christian feast day: Donatus, Romulus, Secundian, and Companions
